is a [science fiction](/wiki/Science_fiction "Science fiction") novelette written by [James Patrick Kelly](/wiki/James_Patrick_Kelly "James Patrick Kelly"), originally published in the June 1995 issue of *[Asimov's Science Fiction](/wiki/Asimov%27s_Science_Fiction "Asimov's Science Fiction")* magazine.

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

1995 science fiction novelette by James Patrick Kelly

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/en/thumb/4/47/Think_Like_a_Dinosaur.jpg/250px-Think_Like_a_Dinosaur.jpg)](/wiki/File:Think_Like_a_Dinosaur.jpg)

"Think Like a Dinosaur" was originally published in the June 1995 issue of *[Asimov's Science Fiction](/wiki/Asimov%27s_Science_Fiction "Asimov's Science Fiction")*

**"Think Like a Dinosaur"** is a [science fiction](/wiki/Science_fiction "Science fiction") novelette written by [James Patrick Kelly](/wiki/James_Patrick_Kelly "James Patrick Kelly"), originally published in the June 1995 issue of *[Asimov's Science Fiction](/wiki/Asimov%27s_Science_Fiction "Asimov's Science Fiction")* magazine.

## Significance

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Think_Like_a_Dinosaur&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Significance")]

The story won the 1996 [Hugo Award](/wiki/Hugo_Award "Hugo Award") for [Best Novelette](/wiki/Hugo_Award_for_Best_Novelette "Hugo Award for Best Novelette"), the [Asimov's](/wiki/Asimov%27s_Science_Fiction "Asimov's Science Fiction") Reader Poll Award, and the SF Chronicle Award. It was also nominated for the [Locus Poll Award](/wiki/Locus_Award "Locus Award") and the [Nebula Award](/wiki/Nebula_Award "Nebula Award"). Since its original publication, "Think Like a Dinosaur" has been reprinted in several science fiction anthologies. In 2001, it was adapted as the "[Think Like a Dinosaur](/wiki/Think_Like_a_Dinosaur_(The_Outer_Limits) "Think Like a Dinosaur (The Outer Limits)")" episode of *[The Outer Limits](/wiki/The_Outer_Limits_(1995_TV_series) "The Outer Limits (1995 TV series)")* TV series. It was adapted into an audio play for [Seeing Ear Theater](/wiki/Seeing_Ear_Theater "Seeing Ear Theater").[[1]](#cite_note-1)

## Plot

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Think_Like_a_Dinosaur&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Plot")]

The story postulates a transportation device supervised by the Hanen, a dinosaur-like race of aliens that can transmit an exact copy of a person's body to distant planets. The original body is disintegrated once reception at the destination is confirmed. The Hanen, nicknamed "dinos" by their human associates, looks down on humans for being scared of this process, making fun of them for being "weepy". In the story, a woman is teleported to an alien planet, but the original is not disintegrated because reception cannot be confirmed at the time. Reception is later confirmed, and the original, not surprisingly, declines to "balance the equation" by re-entering the scanning and disintegrating device and attempts to escape. This creates an ethical quandary that is viewed quite differently by the cold-blooded aliens who provided the teleportation technology, and their warm-blooded human associates. The story concludes with the narrator, a human intern working on the space station housing the device, agreeing with the Hanen's philosophy and murdering the woman to balance the equation and secure the aliens' continued help in humanity's advancement.

## Publication history

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Think_Like_a_Dinosaur&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Publication history")]

"Think Like a Dinosaur" was originally published in the June 1995 issue of *[Asimov's Science Fiction](/wiki/Asimov%27s_Science_Fiction "Asimov's Science Fiction")* magazine. It was subsequently reprinted.

### Anthologies

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Think_Like_a_Dinosaur&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Anthologies")]

* *[Year's Best SF](/wiki/Year%27s_Best_SF_(Book_1) "Year's Best SF (Book 1)")* (1996, edited by [David G. Hartwell](/wiki/David_G._Hartwell "David G. Hartwell"))
* *The Year's Best Science Fiction: Thirteenth Annual Collection* (1996, edited by [Gardner Dozois](/wiki/Gardner_Dozois "Gardner Dozois"))
* *Nebula Awards 31* (1997, edited by [Pamela Sargent](/wiki/Pamela_Sargent "Pamela Sargent"))
* *The Hard SF Renaissance* (2002, edited by [David G. Hartwell](/wiki/David_G._Hartwell "David G. Hartwell") and [Kathryn Cramer](/wiki/Kathryn_Cramer "Kathryn Cramer"))

### Collections

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Think_Like_a_Dinosaur&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Collections")]

* *Think Like a Dinosaur and Other Stories* by James Patrick Kelley (1997)

## References

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Think_Like_a_Dinosaur&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: References")]

1. **[^](#cite_ref-1)** [Online audio drama of *Think Like a Dinosaur*](https://www.sffaudio.com/?p=19883) (Seeing Ear Theatre, SFFAudio.com)
